item.title: Event 1
item.description: Reflection on “FCAJ Community Day - Conference Call” Event Purpose Share best practices on how to build and apply AI in real-world work Introduce new AWS services, products, and solutions related to AI, data, and cloud infrastructure Inspire through case studies, hackathons, and stories of building real products Connect students, developers, and professionals with experts from the industry Speaker List Huynh Hoang Long - Event host Tran Dai Vi - Event host Together with invited speakers from AWS and the technology community Vy Lam - Senior Business Systems Analyst, VPBank Thao Nguyen - GenAI Engineer, VIB Mai Nguyen - GenAI Engineer, VIB Uyen Le - GenAI Engineer, VIB Anh Pham - Cloud Consultant, G-AsiaPacific Vietnam Thinh Nguyen - DevOps Engineer, FCAJ Tinh Truong - Platform Engineer, GoTymeX Duc Dao - Solutions Architect, Cloud Kinetics Highlights Context Is Everything: Making AI Actually Work for You Why AI fails without context, and what “context” really means From prompt to memory: how AI is evolving (the concept of the Second AI Brain) How better context leads to better results (thinking and practical tips) Career direction sharing, how students can start with AI, and Q&A 36 Hours with LotusHacks – Building UTMorpho from Idea to Reality Why I joined LotusHacks The brainstorming journey from zero to an idea Identifying the problem and shaping the UTMorpho product The pressure of building a product in 36 hours Challenges, failures, and turning points An overview demo of the UTMorpho product Lessons learned and future direction From Edge To Origin: CloudFront as Your Foundation Amazon CloudFront for every workload Cost optimization with Amazon CloudFront Security features Improving reliability with Amazon CloudFront Improving performance with Amazon CloudFront Friendly AI Assistant with Amazon Quick Quick Chat Agent: an AI assistant that helps explore data and analyze insights Quick Flows: create intelligent workflows with natural language, without coding Quick Spaces: collaborative spaces that turn personal insights into team knowledge Quick Sight: build dashboards and reports from raw data using natural language Non-Determinism of “Deterministic” LLM Settings How an LLM chooses the next token Assumption: Temperature = 0 guarantees determinism Reality: inference optimizations can still yield different results Real-world impact Mitigation strategies Enterprise-Grade Multi-Agent System: The Case of Startup Credit Scoring The mismatch between traditional banking systems and startup data Single Agent: when it should and should not be used Multi-Agent Paradigm Blueprint of a “virtual credit committee” Guardrails & Compliance Operational ROI and rollout roadmap What I Learned AI Thinking and Context Context is the decisive factor: AI works effectively only when provided with the right context Second AI Brain: understanding how AI is shifting from single prompts to continuous memory and context Building products under pressure: lessons from the 36-hour hackathon on brainstorming, making fast decisions, and handling failure Architecture and Infrastructure CloudFront for every workload: a clearer understanding of how to optimize cost, security, performance, and reliability with CDN Multi-Agent System: understanding when a single agent is enough and when a more complex multi-agent architecture is needed Guardrails & Compliance: the importance of controlling risk when deploying AI in sensitive domains such as credit scoring Operating AI in Practice Amazon Quick: the Chat Agent, Flows, Spaces, and Sight components help businesses exploit data more effectively Non-determinism of LLMs: recognizing that temperature = 0 does not guarantee fully identical results, and that appropriate mitigation strategies are needed Application to Work Improve AI context: build better processes for providing context when working with AI tools in projects Apply CloudFront: consider using CDN to optimize cost and performance for applications being deployed Experiment with Amazon Quick: pilot Quick Chat/Flows/Sight features for internal data analysis Design multi-agent systems: refer to the virtual credit committee blueprint for problems that require coordination among multiple agents Be mindful of non-determinism: build appropriate tests and validation when using LLMs for tasks that require high stability Experience in the Event Participating in “FCAJ Community Day - Conference Call” was an enjoyable experience that helped me gain new perspectives on AI, cloud infrastructure, and how to build real products.

item.title: Event 2
item.description: Reflection on “FCAJ Community Day” Event Purpose Share best practices for operating cloud systems with AI support Introduce the latest AI agent, voice agent, and DevOps agent solutions from AWS Guide the application of AI to operations, HR, and security system integration challenges Connect the technology community with experts and practical solutions from AWS Speaker List Huynh Hoang Long - Event host Together with invited speakers from AWS and the technology community Truong Tran - AI Solution Sales, Noventiq Steve Tran - CTO/Founder, CloudThinker Trung Vu - CEO, Revve AI Anh Dang - Solution Sales, Noventiq Nghi Danh - AI Engineer, Renova Cloud Kiet Tran - AI Engineer, AWS Student Builder Group Bao Phan - Cloud Engineer, Cloud Kinetics Nguyen Nguyen - Cloud Engineer, Cloud Kinetics Toan Nguyen - AWS Security Builder Highlights Deep Response Engine: From Detection to Autonomous Resolution The complexity of modern cloud operations Moving from alert-driven systems to action-driven systems Overview of the Deep Response Engine architecture Live demo of autonomous incident response Business impact: lower costs and zero-downtime operations Voice Agents: Building Human-Like AI Conversations at Scale The development journey from IVR and chatbots to AI voice agents Main challenges: latency, accuracy, and natural interaction Amazon Nova Sonic and the speech-to-speech foundation model Architecture: telephony, streaming, Bedrock, MCP tools Enterprise use cases, best practices, and live demo AWS DevOps Agent: Your Always-Available Operations Teammate Overview of the AWS DevOps Agent Reduced MTTD and MTTR through AI-driven operations Support for multi-cloud and hybrid environments Bedrock AgentCore and the multi-agent reasoning approach Real-world use cases and deployment demo on ECS AI-Powered Productivity: Workforce Planning for Enterprise Digital transformation challenges in HR at modern enterprises Overview of Amazon Quick and its features for HR Accelerating HR operations through automation Workforce analysis and data-driven insights Strategic human resource planning for enterprise decision-making Building Secure Private MCP Connection with Amazon Quick Introduction to Amazon Quick as an AI assistant platform MCP (Model Context Protocol) and its role in scalability Security challenges in MCP-based integration Private connectivity configuration (VPC) for Amazon Quick Demo and practical implementation lessons What I Learned Operating Systems with AI From alert-driven to action-driven: understanding how modern systems are shifting from merely alerting to automatically handling incidents Reducing MTTD/MTTR: learning how AI agents help shorten detection and resolution time for operations Multi-cloud & hybrid: understanding how AI operations solutions can be applied across multiple environments Voice Agents and AI Communication Speech-to-speech foundation model: learning about Amazon Nova Sonic and the architecture behind voice agents Latency and accuracy challenges: identifying technical factors to consider when building natural conversational experiences Integration architecture: how telephony, streaming, Bedrock, and MCP tools work together AI Applications in HR and Security Amazon Quick for HR: how automation and data analysis can accelerate HR operations Security in MCP: understanding the challenges and how to configure private connectivity (VPC) when integrating AI assistants into enterprise systems Application to Work Apply an action-driven mindset: consider moving some current alert-driven processes to automated incident handling Experiment with voice agents: evaluate the feasibility of applying Amazon Nova Sonic to customer care use cases Integrate the DevOps Agent: pilot the AWS DevOps Agent to support current system operations Apply Amazon Quick for HR: propose testing workforce analytics features for HR operations Prioritize MCP security: refer to VPC private connectivity configuration when integrating AI assistants into internal systems Experience in the Event Participating in “FCAJ Community Day” was a valuable experience that helped me stay updated on the latest AI solutions for system operations, voice interaction, and HR management.

item.title: Proposal: Ước tính chi phí vận hành hệ thống trên AWS
item.description: Ước tính chi phí vận hành hệ thống trên AWS Tóm tắt điều hành Tài liệu này ước tính chi phí AWS cho hệ thống phát hiện và xử lý hàng hóa hư hỏng trong kho vận, dựa trên kiến trúc serverless sử dụng Amplify, Cognito, API Gateway, S3, SQS, Lambda, Rekognition, Textract, DynamoDB, SNS, CloudWatch và IAM. Ước tính được xây dựng cho kịch bản thử nghiệm / vận hành thực tế trong 1 tuần, với khối lượng khoảng 245 ảnh/tuần và ~30 người dùng hoạt động.
